texto: That night, I dream. And when I wake up I remember watching a film with Nannan about a ventriloquist who went mad, his dummy coming to life and speaking for itself. My dream is like the end of the film where the ventriloquist and the dummy are in the madhouse, all these mad devil-faces pressed against the iron bars of the cell door, laughing as the dummy gets up off his chair and walks towards the ventriloquist who screams. The dummy strangles him. I can’t remember in the dream if I was the ventriloquist or the dummy. I’m in a funny mood all day. I don’t say much. I don’t feel like it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dean Lilleyman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “That night, I dream. And when I wake up I remember watching a film with Nannan about a ventriloqu…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, addiction, alcoholism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
